title: Service Layer — Business Logic Implementations

interfaces.name: UserService
methods: registerUser(String userName, Long mobileNo, String emailId, String address, int pinCode, String password): String

interfaces.name: ProductService
methods: addProduct(String prodName, String prodType, String prodInfo, double prodPrice, int prodQuantity, InputStream prodImage): String

interfaces.name: CartService
methods: addProductToCart(String userId, String prodId, int prodQty): String
updateProductToCart(String userId, String prodId, int prodQty): String

interfaces.name: DemandService
methods: addProduct(String userId, String prodId, int demandQty): boolean

implementations.name: UserServiceImpl
implementations.notes: Calls MailMessage.registrationSuccess(email, firstName) on successful registration. Uses IUserConstants.TABLE_USER for table name.

implementations.name: ProductServiceImpl
implementations.notes: When stock is replenished (updateProductWithoutImage), triggers DemandServiceImpl to find waiting customers, sends MailMessage.productAvailableNow(), then clears demand entries. Also cascades delete to usercart on removeProduct().

implementations.name: CartServiceImpl
extra_methods: getProductCount(String userId, String prodId): int  // not in interface
cross_service_calls: new DemandServiceImpl().addProduct(DemandBean) — in addProductToCart() when qty exceeds stock
implementations.notes: addProductToCart() checks current stock; if requested qty exceeds stock, caps at available qty and creates a DemandBean for the shortfall. updateProductToCart() acts as upsert (insert if not present).

implementations.name: OrderServiceImpl
cross_service_calls: new CartServiceImpl().removeAProduct(userId, prodId) — in paymentSuccess() per item
new ProductServiceImpl().sellNProduct(prodId, qty) — in paymentSuccess() per item
new UserServiceImpl().getFName(userName) — in paymentSuccess() for mail
implementations.notes: paymentSuccess() is the main checkout orchestrator: fetches cart → creates TransactionBean → loops over items: addOrder + removeFromCart + decrementStock → addTransaction → sends mail. Tables: orders (transactionId, prodId, qty, amount, shipped=0), transactions (transId, username, datetime, amount).

implementations.name: DemandServiceImpl
implementations.notes: addProduct(DemandBean) delegates to addProduct(userId, prodId, qty). addProduct checks for existing demand entry before inserting (idempotent). Tracks backorders for out-of-stock items.

implementations.name: TransServiceImpl
implementations.notes: Minimal service — single lookup: getUserId(transId) returns username from transactions table.

key_relationships: All impls depend_on DBUtil (utility, connection management)
UserServiceImpl, ProductServiceImpl, OrderServiceImpl depend_on MailMessage (email notifications)
bean_classes: UserBean: email(PK), name, mobile, address, pinCode, password
ProductBean: prodId(PK), prodName, prodType, prodInfo, prodPrice, prodQuantity, prodImage(InputStream/byte[])
OrderBean: transactionId, productId, quantity, amount, shipped(int 0/1)
OrderDetails: orderId, productId, prodImage, prodName, qty, amount, time(Timestamp), shipped
utility_classes: IDUtil: generateId():String — used for product IDs
important_notes: All cross-service calls use direct instantiation (new XxxServiceImpl()) — no DI framework.
OrderServiceImpl.paymentSuccess() is the key orchestration method driving checkout flow across 4 services.
CartServiceImpl.addProductToCart() enforces stock limits and auto-creates demand entries for shortfall.
TransServiceImpl is minimal — only resolves transactionId → userId.
No transaction management (no rollback on partial failures in paymentSuccess).
